enrichment.deterministic_verdicts_preserved: findings=0, dropped_flags=0 (none existed; none overridden) per DESIGN §3.1
enrichment.note: Bare planner abstains replaced by reasoned assessments. CONFIRMED-CORRECT claims drop the abstain and carry an auditable advisory:claims-auditor note. Numbers recomputed before any advisory was raised (c3,c5,c8,c12,c22 recomputed clean; c4/c14 resistance!=resistivity and c23/c10 field tension are genuine, recomputed concerns).

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Abstract '~0.2 cm2/V·s' rounds the body value 0.207 (c3/ev7); p-type sign and mobility magnitude are internally consistent. SKEPTIC: weakest link is single-device, single-geometry Hall; DOMAIN-EXPERT (chemistry/perovskite transport): 0.2 cm2/V·s is the expected order for an undoped 2D Pb-iodide. Reviewed, no over-reach; no numeric verifier covers abstract<->body rounding yet (would be a T2 equality check).
routed_to_human.quote: unambiguously identifying p-type conduction with a Hall mobility of ~ 0.2cm2V

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Abstract '~3e14 cm-2' rounds body 3.29e14 (c3/ev7); consistent. DOMAIN-EXPERT: a 2D sheet density ~3e14 cm-2 is physically reasonable for these films. Reviewed, no over-reach; no T2 abstract<->body numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote: a carrier density of ~ 3x1014 cm-2

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT (with one anchor a code verifier would need). Recompute: for p=3.29e14 cm-2 the sheet Hall coefficient R_H,sheet=1/(q·p)=1.90 Ohm/T; pairing with uH=0.207 cm2/V·s implies a sheet resistance R_s≈9.2e4 Ohm/sq, a sensible value, so the (uH, p) pair is mutually consistent given p_2D=1/(qR_H) and uH=|R_H|σ. METHODOLOGIST/CLAIMS-AUDITOR: no over-reach. A full T0 closure needs the measured R_s (figure-read) to verify uH independently of p; flagged so the pass is auditable rather than silent.
routed_to_human.quote: the measured Hall coefficient corresponds to a mobility uH = 0.207 cm2/V·s and a sheet carrier density p = 3.29 x 1014 cm-2

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Recompute of the stated thickness ratio 300nm/100nm = 3.0 exactly matches the 'approximately threefold' prediction (ev4). METHODOLOGIST: the 'if resistivity unchanged' conditional is correctly scoped. Reviewed, no over-reach; a T0 ratio verifier would pass.
routed_to_human.quote: thickness alone would predict only an approximately threefold increase in resistance between the 1500 rpm and 4000 rpm films if the resistivity were unchanged

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. 1500rpm thickness ~300 nm and lowest resistance ~10 MOhm are figure-read (Fig1a/1f, ev4/ev5) and self-consistent with the spin-speed series (thicker, most connected film = lowest R). SKEPTIC: values are plot reads (~ confidence), not a tension. Reviewed, no over-reach; a numeric verifier would need the digitized Fig-1f trace.
routed_to_human.quote: the resulting film had an average thickness of ~ 300 nm (Figure 1a)

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Thickness series 2000/3000/4000 rpm = 225/165/100 nm is monotonically decreasing with spin speed (recomputed ordering holds), as expected from spin-coating physics (DOMAIN-EXPERT). Reviewed, no over-reach; transcription-only (T1), no numeric verifier yet.
routed_to_human.quote: at 3000 rpm (~165 nm) and 4000 rpm (~100 nm)

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Two-regime Arrhenius with Ea(high-T)=0.11 eV > Ea(low-T)=0.015 eV has the correct physical ordering (activation weakens toward a low-T hopping/freeze-out regime). Cross-check vs SI Fig-6 (ev17): the N=9 low-T ln R-vs-1/T slope m=124 K gives Ea=m·kB=0.0107 eV, same order as the quoted 0.015 eV (kB=8.617e-5 eV/K). METHODOLOGIST: extracting Ea from a local slope is standard. Reviewed, no over-reach; a T0 Ea=slope·kB verifier would need the digitized fit segment to close to 2 sig-figs.
routed_to_human.quote: at high temperatures (T > 140 K), the slope yields Ea ≈ 0.11 eV, whereas at low temperatures (T < 70 K), Ea ≈ 0.015 eV is extracted

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Sign reversal of MR (+10% at 3T at RT -> -9% at 3T at 78.2 K) is figure-read (ev9) and internally consistent with the paper's grain-boundary-scattering narrative. DOMAIN-EXPERT: a temperature-driven MR sign change is plausible in disordered hybrid perovskites. Reviewed, no over-reach. (See c23 advisory for a field-value tension with the 1 T magnitude.)
routed_to_human.quote: Large positive MR of 10% at 3T is observed at room-temperature, whereas large negative MR of approximately -9% at 3T is observed at 78.2 K

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Path-dependent MR (crossing 6-8% vs avoiding 0.05-0.2%, ~30-160x) is consistent with the claim that the grain boundary dominates MR, and the 6-8% crossing value is 'comparable' to the FET's ~10% (c10). METHODOLOGIST: comparing co-fabricated current paths is a sound within-sample control. Reviewed, no over-reach; bounds are figure-read, no numeric verifier yet.
routed_to_human.quote: measurement paths that traversed across the observed grain boundary exhibit a much larger MR of 6-8%; the low resistance current paths that avoided this boundary exhibited a much smaller MR in the range of 0.05-0.2%

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Recompute: 1.5 GOhm / (10-50 MOhm) = 30-150x = 1.48-2.18 orders of magnitude, which brackets the claimed 'roughly two orders' (ev11). No over-reach. A T0 order-of-magnitude verifier would pass.
routed_to_human.quote: At 1V, paths avoiding the grain boundary showed resistances of approximately 10-50 MOhm, whereas paths crossing the boundary showed resistances ... an increase of roughly two orders of magnitude

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT as a clearly-labelled geometric *expectation* ('expected to be approximately 500x'). It is a sum-of-(L/W) aspect-ratio estimate, scoped to 'same thickness and resistivity', and is O(100s) which is plausible for a Hall-bar vs three-terminal channel. METHODOLOGIST: not presented as a measurement. Reviewed, no over-reach; exact closure needs the device L/W dimensions (not in the ClaimGraph).
routed_to_human.quote: the Hall-bar geometry is expected to be approximately 500 times more resistive than the three-terminal channel for the same film thickness and resistivity

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. Recompute: measured uH=0.207 falls inside the cited (PEA)2PbI4 range 0.1-1 cm2/V·s (ev14). DOMAIN-EXPERT (perovskite literature): the comparison family and range are apt. Reviewed, no over-reach; a T5 range-membership check would pass, though the literature range itself is author-asserted.
routed_to_human.quote: undoped 2D lead-iodide perovskites such as (PEA)2PbI4 typically exhibit mobilities on the order of ~0.1-1 cm2/V·s

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT and mutually consistent with c18: ~50 kOhm contact resistance against ~10 MOhm-1.5 GOhm film resistances is indeed <=5% (c18 bound), so 'negligible' is justified. METHODOLOGIST: multi-terminal extraction of R_contact is the correct method. Reviewed, no over-reach.
routed_to_human.quote: The contact resistance extracted from multi-terminal structures averaged ~ 50 kOhm and was negligible compared to the film resistance

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT. The <=5% contact-resistance bound is consistent with the ~50 kOhm value in c17 relative to the MOhm-GOhm film resistances. METHODOLOGIST: three/four-wire measurement is the appropriate technique for this bound. Reviewed, no over-reach.
routed_to_human.quote: Three and four-wire measurements showed that contact resistance never exceeded 5% for any current path

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT as stated: a qualitative XRD ordering ('1500 rpm gives the highest (002) intensity', ev18), consistent with c6 (1500 rpm = thickest, most connected, lowest-R film). DOMAIN-EXPERT: stronger (002) for the best-oriented film is expected. Reviewed, no over-reach; intensity is figure-read, so an absolute-intensity verifier would need the digitized Fig-1e pattern.
routed_to_human.quote: with films spun above 1000 rpm showing clear (002) reflections, with the 1500 rpm film giving the highest intensity

routed_to_human.note: CONFIRMED-CORRECT (chemistry T0 recompute). PbI2: 413 mg / M(461.01 g/mol) = 0.896 mmol vs reported 0.895 (exact). MBA: 228 uL x 0.940 g/mL / 121.18 g/mol = 1.77 mmol vs reported 1.80 (<2% off, consistent with density rounding). Molar ratio MBA:PbI2 = 1.97 ≈ 2:1, the correct stoichiometry for (MBA)2PbI4. Reviewed, no over-reach; a T0 mass/molar-mass verifier would pass on PbI2 and pass-within-tolerance on MBA.
routed_to_human.quote: lead (II) iodide (413 mg, 0.895 mmol; Sigma Aldrich)

routed_to_human.note: GENUINE CONCERN (resistance vs resistivity). The 850x ratio (ev3) is a *device resistance* ratio at 1.5 V for one 4000<->1500 rpm pair. Converting to resistivity removes the 3x thickness factor: rho_4000/rho_1500 = 850 x (100nm/300nm) = 283x ≈ 2.45 orders of magnitude, NOT three. The claim itself is fine (it says resistance), but it is the sole support cited for c14's '>3 orders of magnitude resistivity' reduction (both rest on ev3) - see c14. What would confirm: the measured sheet resistances / resistivities for the full spin-speed series so the 850x and the >1000x resistivity claim can be reconciled.
routed_to_human.quote: the much larger observed increase (R4000rpm/R1500rpm ≈ 850 at 1.5V)

routed_to_human.note: GENUINE OVER-REACH RISK (T4). 'In-plane resistivity reduced by more than three orders of magnitude' (>1000x) rests only on ev3, the 850x *resistance* ratio. Recompute: 850x resistance x (100/300) thickness = 283x resistivity = 2.45 orders < 3. The >3-orders claim is therefore not entailed by its cited evidence for the 1500<->4000 rpm pair; it can only hold if it silently spans a broader morphology range (e.g. the near-insulating sub-1000 rpm / discontinuous films whose resistance is off-scale), which is not quantified in the ClaimGraph. METHODOLOGIST/CLAIMS-AUDITOR: resistance ≠ resistivity and the cited number undershoots. What would confirm: resistivity values (R normalized by geometry+thickness) across the full series.
routed_to_human.quote: the in-plane resistivity was reduced by more than three orders of magnitude, enabling reliable lateral device operation

routed_to_human.note: GENUINE CONCERN (T4, figure-grounded). 'VH(B) linear over the full range with positive slope -> p-type' is asserted qualitatively from Fig-4 (ev13) with no reported fit R^2, residuals, or slope uncertainty. The p-type *sign* conclusion is robust (sign of the Hall slope), but 'linear over the full range' is an eyeball claim. SKEPTIC: a weak nonlinearity or an anomalous/ordinary-Hall mixture would not show at this resolution. What would confirm: a linear fit to the digitized VH(B) trace with reported R^2 and a check that the extracted slope reproduces R_H = 1/(q·p) for the p in c3 (then it promotes to a T0 check).
routed_to_human.quote: VH(B) varies linearly with B over the full range, and the positive slope of VH confirms p-type transport in the film

routed_to_human.note: GENUINE CONCERN, and the paper is appropriately cautious. SI Fig-6 R^2 values are 0.9866 (T^-1), 0.9876 (T^-1/2), 0.9887 (T^-1/3), 0.9893 (T^-1/4) (ev17) - a monotone but trivially small spread (ΔR^2≈0.0027). METHODOLOGIST: R^2 increasing toward the 3D-Mott T^-1/4 exponent is exactly what you'd expect from adding a softer nonlinearity to ANY of these fits over a narrow T window; it is NOT evidence for VRH, and the authors correctly decline to claim a mechanism. The genuine concern is only that R^2-ranking is the wrong discriminator here (all four fits are near-degenerate). What would confirm a mechanism: a wider low-T range, a reduced-activation-energy / w = d ln R / d ln T plot, or a physically sensible characteristic temperature T_0 from the T^-1/4 slope. No over-reach in the claim as written; routed as a method note.
routed_to_human.quote: the present data do not show an obvious improvement in linearization ... and thus do not conclusively distinguish a VRH mechanism from Arrhenius-type transport

routed_to_human.note: GENUINE CONCERN (internal field-value tension with c10). Here MR ≈ 10% at |B| ~ 1 T (RT, ev9); c10 reports +10% at 3 T (RT) from the same FET data. If MR is ~10% at both 1 T and 3 T, MR(B) has essentially saturated by 1 T - possible, but the two statements quote the same magnitude at a 3x-different field, so one of the field labels (1 T vs 3 T) is loose. The literature-comparison conclusion ('comparable to other hybrid lead-halide perovskites') is unaffected. What would confirm: the MR(B) curve value at exactly 1 T vs 3 T (figure-read) to verify the field at which |dR|/R reaches 10%.
routed_to_human.quote: the magnitude of the low-field MR in our devices (|dR|/R ~ 10% at |B|~ 1 T) is comparable to that reported for other hybrid lead-halide perovskites

routed_to_human.note: Priority/significance claim: 38 K is asserted as 'the lowest temperature reported for in-plane transport in this material', vs prior chiral-HOIP FET studies at 77-78 K (ev8). That 38 K < 78 K is arithmetic, but whether being the lowest-temperature measurement is *significant/important* - and whether the 77-78 K prior bound is the true state of the art - is a literature-survey + value judgment. Not scored (DESIGN §3.5); needs a domain expert's read of the prior-art landscape.
routed_to_human.quote: in prior temperature-dependent chiral-HOIP FET studies measurements were reported down to 77-78 K

routed_to_human.note: Novelty/priority claim: 'the first Hall-effect characterization of R-(MBA)2PbI4 thin films under dark conditions' (no evidence_refs - a self-asserted first). A 'first' is irreducibly a prior-art question, not a recomputable one. Not scored (DESIGN §3.5); route to a human/literature search to confirm no prior dark Hall measurement on this material exists.
routed_to_human.quote: these measurements represent the first Hall-effect characterization of R-(MBA)2PbI4 thin films under dark conditions
